_publications._publicationTitle: Global proteomics and affinity mass spectrometry analysis of human Schwann cells indicates that variation in and loss of neurofibromin (NF1) alters protein expression and cellular and mitochondrial metabolism.

_contextSnippet: We identified 148 proteins that were significantly altered in spectral count abundance in the WT and null ipn97.4 cells; 68 proteins are increased and 76 proteins are decreased
details: 148 proteins significantly altered in NF1 knockout vs wild-type cells (68 increased, 76 decreased)
